data.title: As an implementation-plan agent, I can annotate task descriptions with cited reference documentation relevant to the task topic
data.description:
## Description

**What**: A ref-library-loader.sh script and its integration into implementation-plan SKILL.md to inject relevant reference citations into task descriptions.
**Why**: Implementation-plan agents currently cite only generic guidance. When an org has WCAG accessibility guidelines, OWASP patterns, USWDS design guidance, or AWS infrastructure docs configured, relevant tasks should automatically carry cited excerpts so implementers act on org-specific best practices, not just general knowledge.
**Scope**:
- IN: ref-library-loader.sh — reads the reference URL list from dso-config.conf (confluence.reference.url / confluence.reference.tags key pairs); applies coarse topic-tag filter (substring match of tags against task title and description); invokes LLM relevance judgment (inline prompt call) on the filtered subset; annotates matching tasks with "> Reference: [Doc Title](url) — [one-sentence excerpt]"
- IN: Tasks with no matching references are generated without annotation and no error is raised
- IN: implementation-plan SKILL.md calls ref-library-loader.sh during task description generation and incorporates any citations into the output task body
- IN: All URL fetching routes through confluence-fetch.sh (Story B) — no independent auth logic in ref-library-loader.sh
- OUT: Confluence researcher, URL fetching infrastructure (Story B owns those)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ref-library-loader.sh reads the reference URL list from dso-config.conf, applies coarse topic-tag filter (substring match), invokes LLM relevance judgment on the filtered subset (capped at configurable maximum, default 20), and annotates matching tasks with citations in the format "> Reference: [Doc Title](url) — [one-sentence excerpt]"
  ← Satisfies: "Tasks matching at least one configured topic-tag filter AND passing LLM relevance judgment are annotated with inline citations"
- When this story is complete, tasks with no matching references are generated without any citation annotation and no error is raised
  ← Satisfies: "Tasks with no relevant references are generated without citations and no error is raised"
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan SKILL.md calls ref-library-loader.sh during task generation and the resulting citations appear in the task description output
  ← Satisfies: "When /dso:implementation-plan generates tasks... tasks... are annotated with inline citations"
- When this story is complete, the reference URL list can be extended by adding new confluence.reference.url / confluence.reference.tags entries to dso-config.conf with no code change
  ← Satisfies: "Adding a new reference requires only a config change with no modification to plugin code"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] LLM relevance filter invoked on an uncapped number of reference URLs may exhaust the context window — cap the post-tag-filter candidate set at a configurable maximum (default: 20) before the LLM call
- [Maintainability] ref-library-loader.sh must call confluence-fetch.sh for all URL fetching — no independent auth, cache, or curl logic
- [Testing] citation injection must be testable via stub fixture — no live Confluence or LLM calls in integration tests (use pre-fetched fixture responses matching the local HTTP stub from Story A)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
